conflict: Cliff's financial desperation driving him to Martin's safe, where he finds something far more valuable than money
consequence: Cliff holds the most dangerous secret in the house — leverage that he does not yet understand is lethal
must_land_beats: Cliff's financial motive: Martin owed him forty thousand from a failed joint real estate deal
The safe: wall-mounted, combination lock, Martin left the dial one tick off
The manila envelope labeled RT — Cliff expects real estate documents
Martin's handwritten account of the 2002 cabin fire in Washburn County, Wisconsin
The account names Tommy as the one who started the fire; names Ray Toomey as the caretaker who died
Cliff reads it twice, puts it back, and the reader should feel the temperature change
pressure_source: Cliff's financial desperation driving him to Martin's safe, where he finds something far more valuable than money
secondary_character_beats: Cliff's financial panic should be legible as genuine desperation, not greed — his business is failing and this money matters

setups_to_plant.description: The handwritten account of the 2002 fire — Martin's record of Tommy starting the fire that killed Ray Toomey. Cliff finds it, photographs it, and replaces it. This document drives the murder motive and the resolution.

state_shift: Cliff holds the most dangerous secret in the house — leverage that he does not yet understand is lethal
